Requests Help
Overview
Settings
General Forms Notifications Catalog Statuses Users Permissions Groups Backups
Requests Settings Guide

Settings Guide

How to configure the Requests portal — patron messages, form fields, notifications, catalog connections, statuses, users, and more.

Overview

Settings are only accessible to Admin users. Go there from the navigation bar or directly via Settings in the nav. The sidebar inside Settings lets you jump between these areas: General, Forms, Notifications, Catalog, Statuses, Users, Permissions, Groups, and Backups.

Most changes take effect immediately after saving. Messaging changes are visible to patrons as soon as you save.

General

The General page is a single form covering eight groups of settings: External Links, ILL, Messaging, Ordering, Patron, Processing, Request Limits, and Staff.

External Links

URL templates the app uses to generate links into external tools. Use the placeholder tokens shown below each field.

SettingWhat it does
ILL ISBN Lookup URLLink to look up ILL items by ISBN (e.g. WorldCat). Use {isbn} as the placeholder.
Polaris Leap Patron URLURL for viewing a patron in Polaris Leap. Use {PatronID} as the placeholder.
SFP ISBN Lookup URLLink to look up SFP items by ISBN (e.g. Amazon). Use {isbn} as the placeholder.

ILL

SettingWhat it does
Enable ISBNdb enrichment for ILLWhen enabled, ILL requests for books/audiobooks search ISBNdb to verify ISBN and add publisher, edition, and other enrichment data. Patrons can confirm a match before submitting.
ILL Age ThresholdIf the item's publish date is older than this many days, the patron sees the ILL Warning Message during submission. Default: 730 days (2 years).
ILL Warning MessageShown to patrons when their item is older than the threshold. Supports rich text formatting.

Messaging

Messages patrons see at various points during submission or when viewing their requests. All support rich text (bold, links, etc.).

SettingWhen it appears
Auto-Order Author Exclusion MessageShown when a patron submits a future release by an author on the auto-order exclusion list — no request is created.
Catalog Owned MessageShown when the patron confirms the catalog already has the item — no request is created.
Duplicate Request MessageShown when a different patron has already requested the same title.
Duplicate Self-Request MessageShown when a patron submits something they've already requested themselves.
ILL Limit Reached MessageShown when a patron hits their ILL request limit. Supports {limit} and {period} tokens.
ILL Limit Until MessageShown below the ILL limit message when a reset date is known. Supports {until} token.
Limit Reached MessageShown when a patron hits their SFP request limit. Supports {limit} and {period} tokens.
Limit Until MessageShown below the limit message when a reset date is known. Supports {until} token.
Submission Success MessageShown after a patron's request is successfully submitted.

Ordering

SettingWhat it does
Auto-Order Author ExclusionsA plain-text list of author names, one per line. When a patron submits a title with a future release date and the author matches this list, the portal shows the exclusion message instead of creating a request. Useful for high-demand authors whose new titles you always pre-order automatically.
💡
The auto-order exclusion only triggers when the item has a specific future release date (like 2025-09-15) — not just a year. Vague dates won't trigger it.

Patron

SettingWhat it does
Enable Patron PIN LoginWhen on, patrons can sign in with their library card PIN at /my-requests to view and track their submitted requests.
Enable Polaris Barcode CheckWhen enabled, Step 1 of the request form checks the patron barcode against Polaris before proceeding. If the barcode is not found, the form stops and shows the message below.
Polaris Barcode Not Found MessageShown on Step 1 when the patron's barcode is not found in Polaris. HTML is allowed — you can include links and your library's address.

Processing

Controls how the portal handles catalog and ISBNdb lookups after a patron submits a request.

ValueBehavior
waitThe patron's browser waits on the page while catalog and ISBNdb lookups run. Fastest feedback for the patron.
emailThe patron sees a confirmation immediately and lookups run in the background. A follow-up email is sent when processing completes.

Request Limits

SFP and ILL limits are configured independently. Each has its own count, window type, and window length.

SettingWhat it does
SFP request limits
SFP Request Limit CountMaximum number of SFP requests a patron can submit within the limit window. Leave blank for unlimited.
SFP Limit Window TypeRolling — counts within a sliding day window; Calendar Month — resets on a fixed day each month; Calendar Week — resets every Monday.
SFP Rolling Window LengthOnly shown when Rolling is selected. The number of days in the sliding window.
SFP Monthly Reset DayOnly shown when Calendar Month is selected. Day of the month the counter resets (1–28).
ILL request limits
ILL Request Limit CountMaximum number of ILL requests a patron can submit. Leave blank for unlimited.
ILL Limit Window TypeSame options as SFP: Rolling, Calendar Month, or Calendar Week.
ILL Rolling Window LengthDays in the ILL rolling window. Used only when ILL Limit Window Type is Rolling.
ILL Monthly Reset DayDay of the month the ILL counter resets when using Calendar Month. Must be between 1 and 28.
💡
When a patron hits their limit, both the submission form and the My Requests view show a message indicating when they can submit again.

Staff

SettingWhat it does
Enable Request AssignmentAllow staff to claim and reassign requests. When enabled, viewing an unassigned request auto-claims it. Rerouting changes field values and unassigns so the next group can claim.
ILL Access Group IDThe selector group whose members may view and work the ILL queue. Set automatically by the seeder but can be changed here if your ILL group changes.
Open Staff Access to RequestsWhen enabled, all staff users can view all requests (SFP + ILL), regardless of their selector group assignments.
Strict Selector Group ScopingWhen enabled (and Open Staff Access is off), selector users can only view SFP requests that match their group's material types and audiences exactly.

Forms

The Forms page controls which fields appear on the Suggest for Purchase and Interlibrary Loan patron submission forms — their order, visibility, required state, and any conditional logic. Two tabs switch between the SFP and ILL field lists.

Field list columns

ColumnMeaning
OrderThe position this field appears in the form. Drag to reorder.
LabelThe label shown to patrons on the form.
KeyThe internal field key (e.g. title, author).
TypeThe input type: text, textarea, select, radio, date, checkbox, etc.
TokenThe placeholder token you can use in email templates to include this field's value (e.g. {title}).
ConditionIf shown, this field only appears when a condition is met (e.g. a specific material type is selected). Fields marked conditional have logic configured in their edit form.
RequiredWhether the field is required before the patron can advance.
ActiveInactive fields are hidden from the form entirely.

Editing a field

Click Edit on any row to change the field's label, required state, active state, step assignment, and conditional logic. Use + New Field to add a custom field to either form.

💡
The SFP and ILL tabs configure each form independently. A field can have different settings (label, required, visibility) on the SFP form versus the ILL form.

Notifications

Controls all automated emails — to selectors when new requests arrive, and to patrons when their request status changes. The Notifications page has two tabs: General and Emails.

General tab

SettingWhat it does
Enable NotificationsGlobal on/off for all emails. Disables everything without touching individual settings.
Email Preview EnabledWhen on, a preview modal appears every time a status change would send a patron email. Selectors can review — and optionally skip — the message before it goes out.
Email Editing EnabledOnly available when Email Preview is also on. Allows selectors to edit the To, Subject, CC, BCC, and body before sending. Changes apply to that send only — templates are not modified. Automatically disabled if you turn off Email Preview.
Email Footer TextText shown in the footer of every notification email sent to patrons and staff. Supports rich text and all field tokens.
💡
Enable Email Preview without Email Editing if you want selectors to see what will be sent but not change it. Enable both if you want selectors to be able to adjust messaging on a case-by-case basis.

Emails tab

The Emails tab is where all email templates live — both Staff routing templates (sent to selector groups when a new request arrives) and Patron status templates (sent to patrons when their request status changes). Use + Staff template or + Patron template to add new ones.

Staff routing templates

When a patron submits a new request, the portal sends a routing email to the matching selector group(s). The default routing template is used for any group that doesn't have its own per-group template.

FieldWhat it does
NameInternal label — not shown in the email itself.
SubjectThe email subject line. Supports tokens like {title}, {material_type}.
Triggered byThe selector group(s) this template applies to. The default staff template applies to all groups that don't have their own.
EnabledUncheck to disable without deleting.

Patron status templates

When you update a request to a status that has Notify Patron enabled, the portal looks for a matching patron template and sends it. The Default patron template acts as a catch-all when no status-specific template matches.

FieldWhat it does
Template nameInternal label — not shown to patrons.
SubjectThe email subject line. Supports tokens like {title}, {patron_first_name}.
Email BodyRich-text editor (Trix). Supports all tokens. Use the Insert buttons below the editor to add tokens at the cursor position.
Triggered by statusesWhich statuses send this template. Only statuses with Notify Patron turned on will actually fire.
Material typesOptional. Leave empty to match all material types, or pick specific ones so this template only sends for those formats.
Default templateOnly one template can be the default. It is used when no other template matches the request's status.
EnabledUncheck to disable without deleting.
💡
Available tokens for emails: {title}, {author}, {patron_name}, {patron_first_name}, {material_type}, {audience}, {status}, {submitted_date}, {request_url}, and many more. Use the Insert buttons below each field to add tokens without typing.
💡
Default templates are seeded for On Order, Purchased, Denied, and ILL Referred. Re-running the seeder will not overwrite templates that already exist.
💡
To control which statuses trigger a patron email, edit the status under Settings → Statuses and check Notify Patron.

Catalog

Controls how the portal connects to your catalog and external metadata sources during patron submission.

Catalog Search

SettingWhat it does
Enable Catalog SearchWhen on, the portal searches the catalog during submission so patrons can be redirected to place a hold if the item is already owned.
Bibliocommons Library SlugYour library's subdomain on Bibliocommons (e.g. dcpl for dcpl.bibliocommons.com).
Catalog Search URL TemplateThe URL pattern used to link patrons to catalog search results. Uses {slug} and {query} as placeholders.

ISBNdb

SettingWhat it does
Enable ISBNdb SearchWhen on, the portal looks up the title in ISBNdb after submission to enrich the record with ISBN, publisher, and publication date. Only runs when the catalog search returns no results.

BiblioCommons Format Labels

Bibliocommons returns raw format codes (like BK or MUSIC_CD) in catalog results. This table lets you map those codes to friendlier display labels shown to patrons (e.g. BK → Book). Click Remove to delete a mapping, or use the form at the bottom to add a new one.

Statuses

Statuses are the labels you apply to requests as you process them — for example Pending, On Order, or Purchased. You can customize them to match your workflow.

Status fields

FieldWhat it does
NameThe label shown in the interface, e.g. On Order.
ColorThe badge color used to visually distinguish this status in lists and detail pages. Use the color picker or type a hex code.
DescriptionA plain-language explanation of what this status means. Shown to patrons on the My Requests page so they understand where their request stands.
Sort OrderControls the order statuses appear in dropdowns. Lower numbers appear first.
Notify PatronWhen checked, transitioning a request to this status triggers a patron email (if notifications are enabled and the patron has an email address). Pair this with a patron email template for that status.
TerminalMark this if the status represents a final state (e.g. Purchased, Denied). Terminal statuses can be used to filter out "closed" requests.
ActiveOnly active statuses appear in the Update Status panel. Inactive statuses are hidden from selectors but preserved in history.
💡
Default statuses (Pending, Under Review, On Order, Purchased, Denied, ILL Referred) are seeded automatically with descriptions and Notify Patron enabled. You can rename, recolor, or reorder them freely.

Users

Staff members who log in via Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) are added here automatically the first time they sign in. You don't need to create accounts manually — just have staff log in and then set their role and group assignments here. The three available roles are Admin, Selector, and Staff (view-only).

Roles at a glance

RoleAccess
Admin Full access to all SFP and ILL requests, patrons, titles, and every Settings page. Can take all workflow actions (status changes, reassignment, routing, deletion).
Selector Sees only the SFP requests matching their assigned selector group(s). ILL access is group-based. Can take workflow actions on requests visible to them, but has no access to settings or user management.
Staff Read-only access to all SFP requests, ILL requests, patrons, and titles. Cannot change statuses, claim or assign requests, merge patrons, or take any write actions. Useful for circulation / reference staff who need to look things up without processing them.

For a fuller explanation of what each role can and can't do, see the Permissions page (below).

Editing a user

Click Edit on any user to change their role, assign them to selector groups, or toggle their account active/inactive. Setting a user to Inactive prevents them from logging in without deleting their record.

💡
A selector with no group assignments can log in but won't see any requests in their queue. Always assign at least one group after changing a user's role to Selector.

Permissions

The Permissions page is a read-only reference describing what each role can see and do in the portal. Use it as a quick explainer when onboarding new staff or deciding which role to assign to someone. Actual role assignment still happens on the Users page.

Administrator

Full access to every part of the portal — all SFP and ILL requests, every patron, every title, every Settings page (including Users, Groups, Statuses, email templates, and Backups). Admins can take every workflow action, including deletion.

Selector

Sees only the SFP requests that match their assigned selector group(s). ILL visibility is group-based — members of the designated ILL group can also view and work the ILL queue. Selectors can take workflow actions on requests visible to them but have no access to settings or user management.

Staff (view only)

Read-only access to all SFP requests, ILL requests, patrons, and titles. Staff can look things up but cannot change statuses, claim or assign requests, merge patrons, or take any other write action. No access to settings or user management. This is the right role for library staff who need to answer patron questions about their requests without being part of the selection workflow.

💡
The page shows a live count of how many active users are in each role, and links straight to the Users page so you can adjust role assignments in one click.

Groups

Groups control which requests each selector sees. A group is a combination of material types and audiences — selectors assigned to a group only see requests that match both.

For example, a group called Adult Books might include the Book material type and the Adult audience. Selectors in that group would only see adult book requests — not DVDs, not teen materials, etc.

💡
The ILL access group is special — its members can view and work the ILL queue. Set which group this is under Settings → General → Staff → ILL Access Group ID.

Creating or editing a group

  1. Click + New Group (or Edit on an existing one).
  2. Give it a name and optional description.
  3. Check the material types and audiences this group should cover.
  4. Leave Active checked so it appears in user assignment dropdowns.
  5. Click Create Group (or Save Changes).
💡
A selector can belong to multiple groups. Their request queue will show requests matching any of their assigned groups.
⚠️
If you deactivate a group, selectors assigned to it lose that scope from their queue. Reassign them to another group if needed.

Backups

The Backups page provides tools for exporting, importing, and restoring data. It is divided into four areas: Configuration, Database & Storage, Server Backups, and Schedule & Retention.

Configuration

Export or import a JSON snapshot of all non-transient configuration — everything needed to restore a fresh install to the same state, without including patron requests, patrons, or titles. Use this to clone a configuration to another environment or to recover after accidental changes.

The configuration export includes:

What's includedDetails
SettingsAll application key/value configuration pairs.
Request statusesName, color, icon, sort order, action label, SFP/ILL scope, terminal state, patron notification flag, advance-on-claim, and description.
FormsThe SFP and ILL form definitions (used as reference points for field configuration).
FieldsAll form field definitions — type, label, scope (SFP/ILL/both), sort order, required, token inclusion, filterable, and conditional visibility rules. Options for select and radio fields (e.g. material types, audiences, genre, console) are included inline.
Form field configPer-form overrides for each field — visibility, sort order, required state, step assignment, label override, and conditional logic.
Form field option overridesPer-form label, visibility, and order overrides for individual field options.
Selector groupsName, description, active state, and linked material type and audience slugs.
Catalog format labelsBiblioCommons format code → display label mappings.
Staff routing templatesPer-selector-group email subject and body.
Patron status templatesSubject, body, linked statuses, and linked field options (material types) for patron notification emails.
ActionWhat it does
Export ConfigDownloads the full configuration as a .json file.
Import ConfigUpload a previously exported JSON file. Existing records are upserted by slug or name — nothing is deleted. Old backup files that used a legacy format are still supported.
💡
On a fresh install, run migrations and the seeder first, then import a config backup. The import will update seeded defaults with your saved values rather than creating duplicates.

Database & Storage

Full data export and restore tools. These include transient data — requests, patrons, and titles — and should be used for full disaster recovery.

ActionWhat it does
Export DatabaseDownloads a full SQL dump of all tables. MySQL-specific — useful for direct server restores on the same database engine.
Export StorageDownloads a ZIP of the storage/app directory — uploaded files and stored assets. Must be extracted manually to restore.
Import DatabaseRestore the database from an uploaded .sql file. Replaces all existing data.
💡
Scheduled and server-saved database backups use a database-agnostic JSON format that can be restored on any supported driver (MySQL, SQLite, etc.). The Export Database button still produces SQL for direct server-level restores.
⚠️
The Wipe Everything option permanently truncates all tables. You must type WIPE to confirm. This cannot be undone — export a backup first.

Server Backups

Use Save to Server Now to write a config JSON and/or database JSON backup directly to storage/app/requests-backups on the server — no browser download required. Files saved here appear in the Server Backups list below, where you can download, restore, or delete them. Server-saved database backups are in JSON format (database-agnostic).

Schedule & Retention

Set how many days to keep server-side backups before pruning, and use the cron schedule builder to generate a command you can add to your server's crontab. The artisan command php artisan requests:backup accepts --config, --db, --storage, --prune, and --all flags. Database backups written by the command are JSON.
